title: Add CMS-style content system and calendar for haunt events, zones, shows, and hayrides with shared operating dates

requirements.text: Create a backend CMS-style content system that supports creating, reading, updating, and deleting (CRUD) four content types: Events, Scare Zones, Shows, and Attractions (for hayrides). Each item must include a name and an English description field.

quotes: Yes like a calendar, add details for each zone and Add larger content system
Own special fields, yes same operation as haunt, add description

acceptanceCriteria: Backend canister exposes CRUD methods for each content type (Events, Scare Zones, Shows, Attractions).
Each content item persists across reloads/upgrades using stable storage.
Each content item includes at minimum: id, name, description.

requirements.text: Support custom fields per content type (do not force one shared schema). Implement distinct, type-specific fields for Events, Scare Zones, Shows, and Attractions, in addition to the shared fields (name, description, dates).
acceptanceCriteria: Each content type has at least one field that is not present on the other types (e.g., Scare Zone location, Show runtime, Attraction ride length, Event organizer).
Backend validation rejects updates that attempt to set fields not applicable to the item’s type.
Frontend forms display and submit the type-specific fields correctly.

requirements.text: Implement a ‘main haunt operating schedule’ in the backend and allow all content items to inherit/sync their dates from this schedule by default (“same operation as haunt”). Provide a way to override dates per item when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores a single main haunt operating schedule record (including at minimum a start date and end date).
Newly created content items default to inheriting the main haunt schedule dates.
If an item is set to inherit schedule dates, changing the main haunt schedule updates the item’s effective dates.
If an item is set to override, its dates remain unchanged when the main haunt schedule changes.

requirements.text: Seed the CMS with the following initial items (names exactly as specified) and ensure they are editable from the CMS UI: Events: “Scream break” (April), “Purge” (May), “east coast” (2027), “Transworld” (2027); Attraction/Hayride: “Farwell haunted hayride”; Scare Zone: “Grave Hell”; Show: “Lost spirits” (coming 2027).

quotes: Make me a event called Scream break in April, and Purge in may, and east coast in 2027, and Transworld in 2027 and a hayride called Farwell haunted hayride, and a new scare zone called Grave Hell, a new show called Lost spirits coming 2027

acceptanceCriteria: After deploy, all listed items exist in storage and appear in the CMS UI.
Each seeded item has an English description value (can be placeholder text but must be editable).
Items that only specify month/year still have dates represented in the system (e.g., as a date range within that month/year) and are editable.

requirements.text: Add a frontend admin/editor experience (CMS UI) that allows authenticated users to add, edit, and delete Events, Scare Zones, Shows, and Attractions, including editing descriptions, type-specific custom fields, and the inherit/override schedule setting.

quotes: Own special fields, yes same operation as haunt, add description

acceptanceCriteria: There is a CMS screen reachable via the existing hash-based routing (same routing style as current pages).
CMS UI supports list + create + edit + delete for all four types.
CMS UI includes controls for: name, description, type-specific fields, and inherit/override dates.
Unauthenticated users cannot perform write operations (UI blocks and backend rejects).

requirements.text: Add a calendar view in the frontend that displays scheduled items with dates. Include Events and also include other item types (Scare Zones, Shows, Attractions) when they have effective dates (inherited or overridden).
acceptanceCriteria: A calendar screen exists and can be navigated to via hash route.
Calendar shows items on their effective dates (inherited from haunt schedule or overridden per item).
User can filter the calendar by content type (Events / Scare Zones / Shows / Attractions).
Selecting an entry shows its details (name, description, and its type-specific fields).

requirements.text: Wire the frontend to the backend using React Query via the existing actor hook so that CMS lists, detail views, and calendar data are loaded from the canister (not hard-coded).
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend uses the existing actor pattern (useActor) and React Query to fetch CMS and calendar data.
Mutations (create/update/delete) invalidate/refetch relevant queries so UI updates without manual refresh.
If the backend is unavailable or returns an error, UI shows an English error state and does not crash.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional backend services).
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose existing shadcn components instead).
Do not edit frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (they are immutable).
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: No third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity if authentication is needed).
No external databases or off-chain storage integrations.
No requirement to generate or modify images for these new CMS items unless separately requested.
